Title: Integrative phenomics, metabolomics and genomics analysis provides new insights for deciphering the genetic basis of metabolism in polished rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor; seed coat color regulator
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds at grain-filling stage; rice grains/polished rice context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: flavonoids; catechins; epicatechins; epicatechin O-hexoside; procyanidin B2; procyanidin B3; diacylglycerophospholipids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to plant defense and have human health-promoting activities; lipids contribute to rice growth and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of multiple flavonoids and diacylglycerophospholipids in polished rice grains; loss-of-function reduces ddA42, dA270, flavonoids and lipids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/hGWAS colocalization; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; metabolite profiling; hyperspectral profiling; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rc colocalized with hyperspectral traits ddA42 and dA270 and metabolite traits including epicatechin and PC 18:0/18:0 at a chromosome 7 hotspot. 2. Rc overexpression increased catechins, epicatechins, glycosylated/polymerized flavonoids, and multiple diacylglycerophospholipids, whereas CRISPR mutants showed the opposite. 3. RNA-seq of Rc OE and CRI seeds showed enrichment of flavonoid/phenylpropanoid/fatty acid biosynthesis genes, supporting Rc as a regulator of both flavonoid and lipid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants in normal rice-growing season
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; hyperspectral phenomics; GWAS with 4.3M SNPs; RNA-seq of filling-stage seeds; accession numbers not provided in main text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving grain flavonoid and lipid composition and for exploiting hyperspectral marker-assisted discovery of metabolic loci; caution that natural loss-of-function haplotypes reduce target metabolites
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although Rc is a transcriptional regulator by prior knowledge, the study text frames it as a candidate gene regulating both flavonoid and lipid metabolism through downstream pathway gene expression; included here as a core gene validated in Results.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ZH11 mentioned for construct generation; functional validation not shown in Results
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B1 important for human nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shown to regulate thiamine accumulation in rice grains according to Discussion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dA212 colocalized with thiamine at 1.76 Mb on chromosome 6. 2. The Wx gene at this locus was stated to regulate thiamine accumulation in rice grains. 3. No direct functional validation details were provided in the main Results text.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) construct generation mentioned in Methods
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants in normal rice-growing season
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; hyperspectral phenomics; GWAS with 4.3M SNPs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving grain vitamin B1 content, but evidence in this paper is limited in Results
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned mainly in Discussion/Methods rather than experimentally validated in Results; borderline core gene.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavonol products and promotes accumulation of multiple related flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: kaempferol; quercetin; myricetin; epicatechin; catechin; flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to plant defense and have human health-promoting activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates flavonoid accumulation and increases hyperspectral trait ddA42 in rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hGWAS candidate discovery; haplotype analysis; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-MS product identification; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; metabolite profiling; hyperspectral profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hGWAS identified LOC_Os09g18450 as a ddA42-associated locus on chromosome 9 that was not detected by mGWAS alone. 2. Recombinant OsFLS converted dihydrokaempferol, dihydroquercetin and dihydromyricetin into kaempferol, quercetin and myricetin, confirming flavonol synthase activity. 3. Overexpression increased ddA42 and flavonoid contents, while CRISPR mutants reduced them, demonstrating positive control of grain flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: T1 seeds at grain-filling stage; rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants in normal rice-growing season
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; hyperspectral phenomics; GWAS with 4.3M SNPs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering or breeding to increase grain flavonoids; also demonstrates utility of hGWAS for discovering hidden metabolic loci
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Frameshift haplotypes showed lower ddA42 and flavonoid levels; gene product mass reported as 37 kDa.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds at filling stage; polished grain context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: flavonoids; catechins; epicatechins; epicatechin O-hexoside; procyanidin B2; procyanidin B3; diacylglycerophospholipids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to defense and human health benefits; lipids contribute to plant growth/development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of flavonoid and lipid accumulation in rice grains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/hGWAS colocalization; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; hyperspectral profiling; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of flavonoids, phenylpropanoids and fatty acids/lipids
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes enriched in flavonoid biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid biosynthesis; fatty acid biosynthesis pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression upregulated pathway genes; knockout downregulated pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-independent positive regulator validated as causal for accumulation of multiple grain flavonoids and lipids linked with ddA42 and dA270
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants in normal rice-growing season
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; hyperspectral phenomics; GWAS with 4.3M SNPs; RNA-seq of filling-stage seeds
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for increasing nutritionally relevant flavonoids and certain lipids in rice grains; can be paired with hyperspectral selection pipelines
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural loss-of-function haplotypes contained premature stop/frameshift variants and showed reduced target metabolite and hyperspectral trait levels.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rc is the validated causal gene at a chromosome 7 hotspot associated with ddA42, dA270, epicatechin and PC 18:0/18:0. 2. Rc overexpression increased multiple flavonoids and diacylglycerophospholipids, while CRISPR disruption reduced them. 3. Rc localized to the nucleus and altered expression of genes enriched in flavonoid, phenylpropanoid and fatty acid biosynthesis pathways, supporting transcriptional regulatory control.
